name: Private Screen Recorder (local, pay-once)
pitch: Record your screen to a local file — no upload, no account, no cloud. A pay-once alternative to Loom/Screencastify.
_evidence: Counts are order-of-magnitude estimates from public sources; confirm on the store before building. Incumbents are PAID CLOUD tools that upload every recording — the local + pay-once wedge is genuine and on-brand.
tosNotes: Records the user's own screen via getDisplayMedia; touches no third-party platform or account.
supportNotes: getDisplayMedia/MediaRecorder audio + codec edge cases — scope tight (tab/screen + mic → webm download) to keep support low.

name: Local OCR / Screenshot-to-Text
pitch: Extract text from any image or screenshot, 100% local.
_evidence: Shelf is dominated by FREE local OCR tools (Copyfish, Text Capture OCR, Image-to-Text). No paid incumbent with painful limits — expect gate #2 to fail.

name: Local EXIF / Metadata Remover
pitch: Strip GPS + camera metadata from photos before you share them, 100% local.
_evidence: Real privacy wedge, but the shelf is mostly FREE (Metadata Remover, EXIF Remover, Remove Metadata Online). Pay-once monetization is contested.

name: Local QR Generator + Reader
pitch: Make and scan QR codes locally — static only, no tracking redirects, no dashboard.
_evidence: Big category but the popular extensions are FREE with zero permissions; paid incumbents are web SaaS (dynamic/tracking QR). Pay-once wedge is contested by free clones.
